approach: in-session conductor holds the user channel; hitl at both gates
NOT additive: rewrites 2 frozen judge.feature scenarios (:11, :60) that assert the scalar four-field output; Clearance granted live by the owner, scoped to those two only
shared measurement instrument for every @rubric scenario in every ACED suite -> blast medium; callers keep a one-invoke contract so no suite fixes it locally
reversible: feature branch, PR-flow; freeze re-openable via async flag
self-reference at the impl gate: aced-impl-judge invokes aced-case-judge, the very subject under change -> name it, do not let it grade itself silently

why: Clearance granted live in-session by unional, scoped to TWO frozen judge.feature scenarios only ('invoked for one case it emits the four-field result', 'the output is exactly the four fields'): both assert the scalar four-field output the per-dimension contract contradicts, so no additive path exists. The other nine frozen scenarios stay untouched; structural gherkin-cli diff vs base confirms 2 removed / 0 modified / 9 unchanged. Owner also decided: blindness enforced inside case-judge (callers keep a one-invoke contract); simulator sees Given+When only; and the brief is composed by a deterministic engine rather than the judge's own judgment

cold spec-judge round 1 found the fix carried the same defect it was fixing: an implementation could spawn the blind simulator, DISCARD its return, and simulate-and-score in its own rubric-aware context while passing every blindness scenario. Closed by binding the scored artifact to the returned transcript. Round 2 found blindness bound brief-composition only, leaving retrieval-off-disk open; round 3 recused the engine node from ACED grading (deterministic, node:test-assertable, not LLM-graded) and proved its suite non-binding by ablation

Clearance ratified live by the owner, scoped to two frozen judge.feature scenarios asserting the scalar four-field output; structural gherkin-cli diff confirms 2 removed / 0 modified / 9 unchanged, so no untouched frozen scenario narrowed. Node judge: cold aced-spec-validator ALIGNED at round 3, all bars PASS -- a scalar impl now fails three independent scenarios, a one-context impl fails five; three prior rounds closed the transcript-provenance hole (an impl could spawn the blind context, discard its return, and score its own rubric-aware simulation while passing every blindness scenario), a dropped trigger-output shape, and a PASS-vs-must-not-do contradiction. Node extract-situation: NEW deterministic engine composing the simulator's brief; recused from ACED grading per aced-fit (assertable, not LLM-graded) so it carries no Fit line, following the sdd authoring/spec-gate engine-node precedent; its suite is boolean and node:test-bound, verified by mutation rather than by reading -- 13 of 24 mutations initially survived, all 13 now caught with a control mutation confirming the harness still reports survivors. Owner blessed the scope growth beyond the aced/case-judge touch-set. pnpm verify 21/21 green

cold impl-judge round 1 returned IMPLEMENTATION_PASS false with two blockers, both real. (1) the case-judge body bound only ONE conjunct of a frozen scenario -- 'a triggered must-not-do withholds the top score AND emits a non-passing verdict' -- its wording licensed emitting the top score with PASS no. (2) removing mean_score/std_dev from run's results JSON broke report, an unreconciled sibling consumer that also headlines a cross-suite mean the CR's own new body forbids. The judge additionally found the engine's CLI entrypoint guard uses import.meta.main (Node >=24.2) against an engines floor of >=22, where it is undefined: the script would print nothing and exit 0, and case-judge keyed its BLOCKER on a non-zero exit, so a supported Node would silently hand back an empty brief -- the exact eval-cannot-fail class this CR closes. Guard moved to the portable form already used by align-spec/check-scenario-overlap, covered by subprocess tests, ablation-proven. case-judge now treats an empty brief as a BLOCKER regardless of exit code

an independent non-ACED reader (brought in because the impl-judge invokes the subject and flagged its own independence as degraded) could not refute the blindness claim, but found the fix had severed the Then from the SCORING context as well as the simulating one. Must-not-do guards live only in Then steps; Pass 2 was told to read the rubric and never the Then, so the one hard fail in the frozen contract had no input and would have failed GREEN, invisibly -- the same eval-cannot-fail class this CR exists to close, reintroduced by its own fix. The asymmetry is now explicit: the simulating context is blind, the scoring context reads the whole scenario. Same reader found the engine skipped docstrings wholesale regardless of owning keyword, so a Given's docstring -- routinely the prompt under test -- silently vanished at exit 0 with a non-empty brief, catching neither guard; docstrings now inherit their owning step's fate. Also closed: no boolean-scenario branch (a regression from the prior body, and a contract gap), a trigger outline's N rows collapsing into one INVOKE (engine gained --row; one row is one case), an unnamed dispatch-failure path, an unresolved skill-dir placeholder, and a bare `node` invocation against a Node 22 engines floor. Both frozen suites took only ADDITIVE scenarios (addOnly true, 0 modified) so no second Clearance was required
